info.name: OpusPopuli Backend
info.description:
GraphQL API collection for OpusPopuli backend microservices.

Services:
- API Gateway (:3000) - federated gateway
- Users (:3001) - auth, profiles, activity
- Documents (:3002) - file management, OCR, analysis
- Knowledge (:3003) - semantic search, RAG
- Region (:3004) - civic data, scraping pipeline
- Region Worker (:3005) - async BullMQ job processor
- Structural Analysis Worker (:3006) - async manifest analysis
- LLM Rerank Worker (:3007) - personalized feed nightly batch (#745)

exec: // Run 'Seed CSRF Token' request first to populate csrf_token env var

item.name: API Gateway - Seed CSRF Token (run first!)

exec: console.log('WARNING: Could not extract CSRF token. Headers:', pm.response.headers.toString());

item.name: Auth
item.description:
Authentication flows.

For UAT testing, use 'Login via Supabase Auth' to get a JWT, then all subsequent requests use it automatically via the collection's Bearer auth.

Setup: Run 'Seed CSRF Token' first, then 'Login via Supabase Auth'.

item.name: Login via Supabase Auth (run this first!)
County Representatives
item.description: Get Board of Supervisors for a specific county by county region plugin name.

item.name: My County Supervisors
item.description: Get Board of Supervisors for the authenticated user's county (resolved from their primary address jurisdiction). Requires auth.

item.name: Enable Region Plugin (Admin)
item.description: Enable or disable a region plugin by name. County plugins are disabled by default and must be enabled before syncing. Set enabled: false to disable.

item.name: Invalidate Manifest (Admin)
item.description: Deactivate cached structural manifests for a source URL so the next sync triggers fresh AI re-analysis.

item.name: Pipeline Jobs (Region Worker)
item.description:
Async region sync job management.

syncRegionData now enqueues a BullMQ job and returns immediately. Use the returned jobId to poll status via regionSyncJob, or list recent jobs with recentRegionSyncJobs.

Status values: QUEUED → RUNNING → SUCCEEDED | FAILED

item.name: Sync — Representatives (quick test)
item.description: Light smoke test. Caps at 1 rep so Ollama bio generation is minimal. Saves sync_job_id for use in the poll requests below.

item.name: Sync — State Reps (Admin)
item.description: Sync state-level representatives. depth defaults to STATE. Saves sync_job_id.

item.name: Sync — County Reps, All Counties (Admin)
item.description: Sync Board of Supervisors for all 58 enabled CA county sub-regions. Saves sync_job_id.

item.name: Sync — County Reps, Single County (Admin)
item.description: Sync Board of Supervisors for one county. Set regionId to the plugin name, e.g. 'california-sonoma'. Saves sync_job_id.

item.name: Sync — CIVICS (Admin)
item.description: Trigger a CIVICS data sync. Returns a job ID immediately; the worker processes it async. Saves sync_job_id.

item.name: Sync — PROPOSITIONS (Admin)
item.description: Trigger a PROPOSITIONS data sync (qualified ballot measures). Returns a job ID immediately; the worker processes it async. Saves sync_job_id.

item.name: Sync — MEETINGS (Admin)
item.description: Trigger a MEETINGS data sync (Assembly/Senate daily journals + weekly histories — pdf_archive sources fetch and extract LegislativeActions per #666). Returns a job ID immediately; the worker processes it async. Saves sync_job_id.

item.name: Sync — CAMPAIGN_FINANCE (Admin)
item.description: Trigger a CAMPAIGN_FINANCE data sync (committees, contributions, expenditures from CAL-ACCESS). Returns a job ID immediately; the worker processes it async. Saves sync_job_id.

item.name: Sync — BILLS (Admin)
item.description: Trigger a BILLS data sync (legislative bills + status pages from leginfo). Use maxBills to cap the run for smoke testing; omit for full sync. Returns a job ID immediately; saves sync_job_id.

item.name: Poll Job Status (by sync_job_id)
item.description: Poll the job saved by the last sync mutation. Refresh until status is SUCCEEDED or FAILED.

graphql.query:
query RegionSyncJob($jobId: ID!) {
  regionSyncJob(jobId: $jobId) {
    jobId
    status
    triggerSource
    regionId
    dataTypes
    enqueuedAt
    startedAt
    finishedAt
    elapsedMs
    errorMessage
    results {
      regionId
      dataType
      itemsProcessed
      itemsCreated
      itemsUpdated
      errors
      syncedAt
    }
  }
}

item.name: Recent Sync Jobs
item.description: List the most recent pipeline jobs. Useful for a dashboard view of sync history.

graphql.query:
query RecentRegionSyncJobs($limit: Int) {
  recentRegionSyncJobs(limit: $limit) {
    jobId
    status
    triggerSource
    regionId
    dataTypes
    enqueuedAt
    startedAt
    finishedAt
    elapsedMs
    errorMessage
  }
}

item.name: Scheduled Sync Jobs (Admin)
item.description: List all BullMQ repeatable job schedulers currently registered on the region-sync queue. Each entry shows the scheduler key (e.g. 'california-meetings-cron'), its staggered cron pattern, and the next scheduled fire time as a Unix epoch millisecond value (null if not yet computed). Requires REGION_SYNC_CRON_ENABLED=true on the region-worker for entries to appear.

item.name: Personalized Feed (LLM Rerank Worker)
item.description:
Personalized bill-feed re-rank job management (opuspopuli#745).

triggerMyLlmRerank enqueues a BullMQ job onto the llm-rerank queue and returns immediately with the lifecycle row jobId (UUID of the llm_rerank_jobs row). The llm-rerank-worker (:3007) consumes the queue, runs LlmRerankService.rerankForUser, and writes per-user, per-bill rows to personalized_feed_cache.

In UAT the nightly cron is disabled (LLM_RERANK_CRON_ENABLED=false) — use this mutation to trigger an ad-hoc rerank for the authenticated user. Production runs the cron at 3 AM UTC by default.

Poll job status via myLlmRerankJob(jobId) using the llm_rerank_job_id env var the trigger request saves. Status values: QUEUED → RUNNING → SUCCEEDED | FAILED. The result payload (candidatesConsidered, cacheWrites, validatorRejections, totalTokens, budgetExhausted) is populated on success. Polling is scoped to the authenticated user — you only see your own jobs.

item.name: Trigger My LLM Rerank
item.description:
Enqueue a re-rank job for the authenticated user. Pass the user's interestTags + all 20 ranking flags (the resolver derives the TRUE-only list internally). Saves llm_rerank_job_id from the response.

candidateLimit is optional (defaults to 20 in the worker). Lower it to ~3-5 during local LLM testing so Ollama doesn't run for several minutes per call.

graphql.variables:
{
  "input": {
    "interestTags": ["housing", "healthcare"],
    "flags": {
      "isRenter": true,
      "isHomeowner": false,
      "isParent": false,
      "isCaregiver": false,
      "isStudent": false,
      "isEducator": false,
      "isWorker": true,
      "isBusinessOwner": false,
      "isUnionMember": false,
      "isGigWorker": false,
      "isTransitRider": true,
      "isDriver": false,
      "hasSpecialLicense": false,
      "hasImmigrationConcern": false,
      "hasHealthCondition": false,
      "hasPublicHealthInsurance": false,
      "isVeteran": false,
      "hasJusticeInvolvement": false,
      "isLowIncome": false,
      "receivesPublicBenefits": false
    }
  },
  "candidateLimit": 5
}

item.name: Poll Rerank Job Status (by llm_rerank_job_id)
item.description: Poll the job saved by the last Trigger My LLM Rerank request. Refresh until status is SUCCEEDED or FAILED. Scoped to the authenticated user — returns null if you try to poll someone else's job id.

graphql.query:
query MyLlmRerankJob($jobId: ID!) {
  myLlmRerankJob(jobId: $jobId) {
    jobId
    status
    triggerSource
    candidateLimit
    attempts
    enqueuedAt
    startedAt
    finishedAt
    elapsedMs
    errorMessage
    result {
      candidatesConsidered
      cacheWritesWithExplanation
      cacheWritesWithoutExplanation
      llmFailures
      validatorRejections
      budgetExhausted
      totalTokens
    }
  }
}

item.name: My Recent Rerank Jobs
item.description: List the authenticated user's most-recent rerank jobs. Useful for a dashboard view of rerank history. Limit defaults to 20 and is clamped to 100.

graphql.query:
query MyRecentLlmRerankJobs($limit: Int) {
  myRecentLlmRerankJobs(limit: $limit) {
    jobId
    status
    triggerSource
    candidateLimit
    attempts
    enqueuedAt
    startedAt
    finishedAt
    elapsedMs
    errorMessage
  }
}

item.name: My Personalized Bill Feed (verify cache after rerank)
item.description: Reads the user's personalized feed. After the worker processes the triggerMyLlmRerank job, rows in this response should carry a non-null relevanceExplanation (the LLM-written 'why this matters to you' sentence). Rows without it fell back to embedding-only ranking (LLM declined, validator rejected, or budget exhausted — see myLlmRerankJob result for the per-user summary).

graphql.variables:
{
  "input": {
    "interestTags": ["housing", "healthcare"],
    "flags": {
      "isRenter": true,
      "isHomeowner": false,
      "isParent": false,
      "isCaregiver": false,
      "isStudent": false,
      "isEducator": false,
      "isWorker": true,
      "isBusinessOwner": false,
      "isUnionMember": false,
      "isGigWorker": false,
      "isTransitRider": true,
      "isDriver": false,
      "hasSpecialLicense": false,
      "hasImmigrationConcern": false,
      "hasHealthCondition": false,
      "hasPublicHealthInsurance": false,
      "isVeteran": false,
      "hasJusticeInvolvement": false,
      "isLowIncome": false,
      "receivesPublicBenefits": false
    }
  },
  "limit": 5
}
